commands.repair_reason: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the remaining action is maintainer compatibility judgment plus normal PR checks, not a concrete code defect.; Cleared: The diff does not add dependencies, secret handling, CI execution, or new supply-chain surface; the sentinel-stripping helper reduces accidental marker exposure. (sha=2f5042653d08ee13dde8b2bf5deb548bef653217)
commands.review_summary:
This PR routes hook and system-prompt override runtime context through cache-boundary helpers and adds regression tests for byte-stable prompt-cache prefixes.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main joins hook system context around the marker-bearing base prompt before provider cache splitting, and the PR body supplies live Anthropic/OpenAI before-after cache counter output.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the remaining action is maintainer compatibility judgment plus normal PR checks, not a concrete code defect.

target.title: fix(prompt-cache): preserve prefix across hook/runtime context
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` (4 call sites) and the `systemPromptOverrideText` runtime-extra-context branch wrote hook/runtime context ABOVE the system-prompt cache-boundary marker. The downstream `anthropic-payload-policy.ts` splits on the marker to place `cache_control`, so the per-turn-varying hook content ended up inside the stable-prefix region and invalidated the prompt cache on every turn. OpenAI auto prefix cache hit the same prefix-byte-stability requirement and missed identically. Issue #85203 measured ~50-65% cache hit vs achievable 88-94%.
- Solution: route hook prepend, hook append, and runtime-extra-context additions through the cache-boundary helper so they land in the dynamic suffix region. Add a missing `appendSystemPromptAdditionAfterCacheBoundary` (existing helper covered only prepend) and `ensureSystemPromptCacheBoundary` (idempotent boundary synthesis for top-level prompt assembly boundaries). Both helpers strip embedded marker substrings from additions to prevent a smuggled sentinel reaching providers via the dynamic suffix.
- What changed: 7 files, +342 / -21 lines across 2 commits. Commit 1 (`a100ed525d`) lands the helpers + refactor + base regression tests; commit 2 (`2f5042653d`) adds stripped-bytes lock-in assertions for the two disclosed visible-byte changes (markerless append-only single-newline join; override+extraSystemPrompt single-newline gap between override text and subagent context). New helpers in `src/agents/system-prompt-cache-boundary.ts`; `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` refactored to chain ensure → prepend → append; `appendRuntimeExtraSystemPrompt` (override branch) routed through the same helpers.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Sites 1, 2, 4, 5 from #85203 (they operate on user-turn text, not system prompt) are NOT touched. Site 7 (`appendFileBlocks`) operates on inbound message body, also not touched. Site 6's generated-system-prompt path (the fragment lands in the outer dynamic region below the marker emitted at `system-prompt.ts:1239`) is correct as-is and is NOT touched. Google managed cache paths are out of scope per the issue. The legacy no-marker fallback inside `prependSystemPromptAdditionAfterCacheBoundary` is left unchanged for any pre-existing caller that may rely on it.

## Motivation

The cache-boundary marker was introduced precisely to keep the system-prompt stable prefix byte-identical across turns so providers can hit prompt cache from turn 2 onward. Two paths bypassed it. The cost is measurable: every turn re-creates the cache instead of reading it, multiplying the input-token bill by N for an N-turn conversation and burning provider cache-creation latency on every step.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #85203
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

The issue is not auto-closed because the issue body enumerates 6 dist:line sites; this PR ships the 2 actually-broken system-prompt paths (`composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` and the override branch) plus the missing `appendSystemPromptAdditionAfterCacheBoundary` helper. Sites 1/2/4/5/7 operate on different surfaces (user-turn text or inbound message body) and are out of scope. Maintainers may close #85203 after merge if the analysis above is accepted, or keep it open to track any of the unaddressed surfaces if they choose.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:

The bug class: when `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` was used with a varying hook prepend/append (typical case: session/runtime context that changes per turn), the hook content landed BEFORE the cache-boundary marker in the assembled system prompt. The provider-side `anthropic-payload-policy.ts` splits the system prompt on the marker to decide where to place the `cache_control` breakpoint; with hook content in the stable-prefix region every turn produced a different prefix and the cache missed on turn 2+. OpenAI's auto prefix cache has the same prefix-byte-stability requirement and was broken identically. The `systemPromptOverrideText` runtime-extra-context branch bypassed the helper entirely and emitted a fully markerless prompt, so override + subagent context broke prompt cache on every subagent spawn.

- Real environment tested:

Standalone Node ESM proof script (`/tmp/proof-cache.mjs`) imports `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` and the boundary helpers from BOTH the build of this branch and the build of `upstream/main 6bd430ee35`, sends 3-turn sequences with varying hook content to live provider APIs, and captures cache token counters from real responses. The script reuses the production `applyAnthropicCacheControlToSystem` block-split logic (`splitSystemPromptCacheBoundary` → two-block system with `cache_control` on the stable-prefix block only) so the Anthropic payload shape is identical to what OpenClaw emits at runtime.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```
pnpm -F openclaw build         # V3 worktree (this branch)
pnpm -F openclaw build         # baseline worktree at upstream/main 6bd430ee35
node /tmp/proof-cache.mjs      # imports both dists, hits both providers, prints results
```

- Evidence after fix (terminal capture, copied live output):

Stable-prefix byte equality across the 3 turns (extracted via `splitSystemPromptCacheBoundary` on the composed system prompt):

```
baseline stable_prefix_byte_identical_across_turns = false
baseline stable_prefix_lengths = [22843, 22840, 22841]

V3       stable_prefix_byte_identical_across_turns = true
V3       stable_prefix_lengths = [22736, 22736, 22736]
```

Anthropic `/v1/messages` response usage (cache-eligible Anthropic model, 5-minute ephemeral cache):

```
                  baseline                              V3
turn 1   cache_creation=5045  cache_read=0    cache_creation=5006  cache_read=0
turn 2   cache_creation=5045  cache_read=0    cache_creation=0     cache_read=5006
turn 3   cache_creation=5045  cache_read=0    cache_creation=0     cache_read=5006
```

OpenAI `/v1/chat/completions` response usage (cache-eligible OpenAI model, auto prefix cache):

```
                  baseline                              V3
turn 1   prompt=4840  cached=0               prompt=4840  cached=0
turn 2   prompt=4844  cached=0               prompt=4844  cached=4736
turn 3   prompt=4844  cached=0               prompt=4844  cached=4736
```

- Observed result after fix:

Baseline re-creates the cache on every turn (`cache_creation` on every turn, `cache_read=0` on every turn) on Anthropic and reports `cached_tokens=0` on all 3 turns on OpenAI. V3 writes the cache on turn 1 and reads the full stable prefix from cache on turns 2 and 3 (Anthropic `cache_read=5006`, ~99% of input; OpenAI `cached_tokens=4736`, ~98% of prompt). The stable prefix is byte-identical across all 3 turns and only the dynamic suffix (hook content + dynamic base context) varies.

- What was not tested:
    - Subagent override branch (`attempt-system-prompt.ts`) cache behavior was not exercised against live providers — it is covered by unit tests (`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt-system-prompt.test.ts`) that assert the new byte sequence and marker-aware composition.
    - Google managed cache paths (out of scope per the issue body — those providers use server-side conversation cache, not prompt-prefix cache).
    - Long-TTL (`extended-cache-ttl-2025-04-11`) was not exercised; the proof used the default 5-minute ephemeral TTL because the 3 turns fit comfortably in that window.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Two system-prompt mutation paths bypassed the cache-boundary helper. `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` (4 call sites in the prepare + attempt runners) concatenated hook prepend/append text around the base system prompt with no marker-aware logic, so when the base contained the marker the hook content landed in the stable-prefix region. The `systemPromptOverrideText` branch bypassed the generated system-prompt builder entirely and emitted no marker at all, so override + subagent context was a fully markerless prompt and could never be split for cache_control placement.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression test asserted byte-stable stable prefix across turns when hook context varied. The existing `system-prompt-cache-boundary.test.ts` covered the helper in isolation but not the helper's callers.
- Contributing context: `appendSystemPromptAdditionAfterCacheBoundary` did not exist; only the prepend variant was defined. The override-branch refactor needed both directions to remain readable.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
    - [x] Unit test
    - [x] Seam / integration test
    - [ ] End-to-end test
    - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
    - `src/agents/system-prompt-cache-boundary.test.ts` (helper-level + cache-prefix-stability regression)
    - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts` (interaction: hook prepend + hook append in the same turn through the full compose chain)
    - `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt-system-prompt.test.ts` (override-branch byte sequence + marker-precedes-subagent-context + smuggled-marker-strip)
    - `src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts` (compose invoked with expected argument shape from the prepare call site)
- Scenario the test should lock in: with a marker-bearing base system prompt and a hook prepend that varies per turn, the bytes BEFORE the marker (the stable prefix) must remain byte-identical across turns; smuggled marker substrings inside additions must be stripped so `splitSystemPromptCacheBoundary` (which uses `indexOf` and matches the first marker only) cannot be misled; AND the two intentional post-strip visible-byte changes (markerless append-only single-newline; override+extra single-newline gap) are asserted on the stripped output so a future helper change cannot silently alter the disclosed contract.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: a single-process unit test that compares stable-prefix bytes across two compose calls catches the regression deterministically with no provider/network dependency.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): the helper-level tests in `src/agents/system-prompt-cache-boundary.test.ts` existed but did not assert the prepend+append composition path or the override-branch path. Both gaps are filled.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A — new tests added.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Three intentional, disclosed byte-level changes:

1. Hook prepend/append context now appears AFTER the (synthesized) cache-boundary marker. Previous byte order was `prepend + base + append`; new byte order is `base-stable-region + MARKER + prepend + base-dynamic-region + append`. This is the intended fix — the entire point of routing through the helper is to keep the stable prefix byte-identical across turns. Visible to the model only after `stripSystemPromptCacheBoundary` collapses the marker into `\n`.

2. Append-only paths use a single-newline separator between the stable prefix and the appended addition AFTER the downstream provider strip. The new helper emits `stablePrefix + MARKER + addition` when the input has no dynamic suffix; once `stripSystemPromptCacheBoundary` runs in the provider adapter, the marker collapses to `\n`, so providers see a single newline between the two regions. Previous naive append patterns in this codebase used a literal `\n\n` separator with no marker.

3. Override + extraSystemPrompt visible bytes change from `OVERRIDE\n\n## Subagent Context\n<extra>\n` to `OVERRIDE\n## Subagent Context\n<extra>` because the override branch now routes through `ensureSystemPromptCacheBoundary` + `appendSystemPromptAdditionAfterCacheBoundary`. The override path previously assembled the suffix with literal `\n\n` separators and a trailing newline; the helper-based assembly drops the trailing newline and emits the join via the marker (which the provider then strips to `\n`). Existing tests at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt-system-prompt.test.ts` are updated to reflect the new byte sequence.

## Diagram

```text
Before (baseline):
  [HOOK_PREPEND]──┐
                  ├─► concat ─► [HOOK_PREPEND │ STABLE_BASE ╎MARKER╎ DYNAMIC_BASE │ HOOK_APPEND]
  [BASE w/ MARK]──┤                            ─────────────────┬───────────────  
  [HOOK_APPEND ]──┘                            stablePrefix on split (varies per turn)

After (V3):
  ensure(BASE)         →  [STABLE_BASE ╎MARKER╎ DYNAMIC_BASE]
  prepend(HOOK_PREPEND)→  [STABLE_BASE ╎MARKER╎ HOOK_PREPEND │ DYNAMIC_BASE]
  append (HOOK_APPEND) →  [STABLE_BASE ╎MARKER╎ HOOK_PREPEND │ DYNAMIC_BASE │ HOOK_APPEND]
                          ───────┬──────                     
                          stablePrefix on split (byte-stable across turns)
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A.

Marker-substring sanitization in both helpers (full marker + trimmed comment form) is a defense-in-depth hardening: a hook or extra-context value that happens to contain the sentinel can no longer reach the provider verbatim or fool the downstream `splitSystemPromptCacheBoundary` (which uses `indexOf` and matches only the first marker). No new attack surface is introduced.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.17.0-14-generic
- Runtime/container: Node 22.22.1, pnpm
- Model/provider: provider-agnostic — applies to all providers consuming the system-prompt cache-boundary marker (Anthropic-family + OpenAI-family). Proof script used live cache-eligible models from both families.
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A (system-prompt assembly path).
- Relevant config (redacted): default cache retention (`short` / 5-min ephemeral on Anthropic; auto on OpenAI). `OPENAI_API_KEY` from env; Anthropic via Max OAuth token.

### Steps

1. Build both worktrees (V3 branch + baseline at `upstream/main 6bd430ee35`).
2. Construct a deterministic ~22.7 KB stable base system prompt with the marker emitted at the same position the production builder emits it.
3. For each (version × turn), call `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` from that version's dist with a hook prepend/append that varies per turn (simulating session/runtime hook context).
4. For Anthropic: replicate `applyAnthropicCacheControlToSystem` (split on marker → two-block system, `cache_control: ephemeral` on stable-prefix block only) and POST to `/v1/messages`.
5. For OpenAI: strip the marker and POST the resulting plain text as the system message to `/v1/chat/completions`.
6. Capture `usage.cache_creation_input_tokens` / `usage.cache_read_input_tokens` (Anthropic) and `usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens` (OpenAI) per turn.

### Expected

- Baseline: stable prefix bytes vary per turn (hook content in stable-prefix region) → cache_read = 0 on every turn → cache_creation paid on every turn.
- V3: stable prefix bytes byte-identical across turns → cache_read ≈ full stable-prefix size from turn 2 onward.

### Actual

Matches Expected — see the Real behavior proof section above for full numbers (baseline turn 2/3 cache_read = 0 / cached = 0; V3 turn 2/3 cache_read = 5006 on Anthropic, cached = 4736 on OpenAI).

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [x] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Unit + interaction tests cover the V3-affected code paths: 370/370 passing across `system-prompt-cache-boundary.test.ts`, `attempt.test.ts`, `attempt-system-prompt.test.ts`, `cli-runner/prepare.test.ts`. `pnpm check:changed` (typecheck + lint + import cycles) clean.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
    - Built both V3 and baseline worktrees from clean state, ran the proof script against live provider APIs, observed the cache_read / cached_tokens numbers above.
    - Confirmed stable-prefix byte equality across the 3 V3 turns via `splitSystemPromptCacheBoundary` on the composed system prompt.
    - Ran the 4 V3-affected test files (`system-prompt-cache-boundary.test.ts`, `attempt.test.ts`, `attempt-system-prompt.test.ts`, `cli-runner/prepare.test.ts`); all green after the lock-in assertions were added.
    - Verified `pnpm check:changed` runs clean on the V3 worktree.
- Edge cases checked:
    - Marker-bearing extraSystemPrompt: the smuggled sentinel is stripped by both prepend and append helpers (covered by the new `attempt-system-prompt.test.ts` strip test).
    - Markerless input with empty dynamic suffix: helper emits `stablePrefix + MARKER + addition` (single-newline after provider strip); covered by unit test.
    - Override-without-extraSystemPrompt: remains markerless (no helper invocation when there is no addition).
    - Existing `prependSystemPromptAdditionAfterCacheBoundary` no-marker fallback path: untouched; all V3 callers route through `ensureSystemPromptCacheBoundary` first so they never trigger that legacy fallback.
- What you did **not** verify:
    - Live-provider cache behavior of the override branch (covered by unit-test assertions on byte sequence + marker precedence, not by real provider calls).
    - Long-TTL (1h `extended-cache-ttl-2025-04-11`) — the 3-turn proof window fits comfortably inside the default 5-min ephemeral cache.
    - Google managed cache (out of scope).
    - Wider test suite was not re-run as part of this PR's verification; `pnpm check:changed` + the 4 V3-affected test files were the focused gate. Unrelated pre-existing baseline failures exist on `upstream/main 6bd430ee35` (8 in `compact.hooks.test.ts` + `model.forward-compat.errors-and-overrides.test.ts`); not introduced by this PR.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

(PR not yet opened — this section will be applicable after first bot review pass.)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` (all exported helper signatures preserved; `composeSystemPromptWithHookContext` signature unchanged; the existing prepend helper's behavior unchanged for callers that already passed marker-bearing bases).
- Config/env changes? `No`.
- Migration needed? `No`.

The 3 intentional byte-level visible changes (see User-visible / Behavior Changes) are downstream byte-shape changes inside the system prompt the model receives. They do not change any exported API, config, or env contract.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a third-party plugin or external caller could be invoking `prependSystemPromptAdditionAfterCacheBoundary` directly on a fragment that does not contain the marker, relying on the legacy "prepend-as-fallback" behavior (the helper currently prepends the addition rather than appending it when no marker is found).
    - Mitigation: legacy no-marker fallback in the prepend helper is left untouched. All in-tree callers now route through `ensureSystemPromptCacheBoundary` first, so they never trigger that fallback; external callers continue to get the existing behavior.
- Risk: override-branch byte change (`OVERRIDE\n\n## Subagent Context...\n` → `OVERRIDE\n## Subagent Context...`) could matter for any downstream consumer that pattern-matches the override+subagent prompt as a literal string.
    - Mitigation: existing fixture and the updated `attempt-system-prompt.test.ts` assertions lock in the new shape; the change is disclosed under User-visible / Behavior Changes. If a consumer needs the old shape, the override branch is one helper call away from being adjusted.

---

AI-assisted; contributor manually reviewed and is responsible for the change.
